### exercise  {.smaller}

<!-- adapted from 2025 spring final -->

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```c
struct Process {
    long is_active;
    ...
}
struct Process processes[16384];

void SomeOsRoutine() {
    ...
        struct Process *this_process;
        this_process = &processes[process_id];
        if (this_process->is_active) {
    ...
}
```

::::

:::: {.column}

```c
char *array;
array = AllocateAlignedPhysicalMemory(CACHE_SIZE);
LoadIntoCache(array, CACHE_SIZE);
SomeOsRoutine();
for (int i = 0; i < CACHE_SIZE; i += BLOCK_SIZE) {
    if (TimeAccessTo(&array[i]) > THRESHOLD) {
        /* found something interesting */
    }
}
```

::::

:::::

* 65536 byte ($2^{16}$ byte) direct-mapped data cache with 16-byte blocks
* `processes[0]` is located at address `0x100000`
* `struct Process` is 128 bytes in size
* array index 768 ($48\times 16$) evicted by code above, possible `process_id`?

### solution

* array[X] is cache offset $X \mod 16$, cache set $(X / 16) mod 2^{12}$ (number of sets).
* processes[Y].is_active is cache offset 0, cache set $Y \times (128/16) \pmod{2^{12}}$
    *  128 bytes per struct Process, so each struct Process spans 8 cache sets
* $Y \times (128 / 16) = (768/16) \pmod{2^{12}}$ implies $Y = 6 + K\times2^9$ for some $K$
